name: Impel Down
description: Impel Down is the World Government's maximum-security underwater prison, considered the most fearsome and inescapable jail in the world. Built in the Calm Belt between the Gates of Justice connecting to Enies Lobby and Marine Headquarters, it's a massive structure submerged beneath the ocean with six levels descending deeper underground - each level worse than the last. The prison houses the world's most dangerous criminals, from common pirates in the upper levels to legendary prisoners whose existence has been erased from history in the lowest depths. The entire structure is filled with torture devices, deadly creatures, and environmental hazards designed to break prisoners both physically and mentally. It was considered impregnable until Luffy, aided by Boa Hancock, infiltrated it to rescue his brother Ace, triggering the greatest mass prison break in history. The facility is run by Chief Warden Magellan, whose Poison-Poison Fruit makes him nearly unbeatable within the prison's confines.
history: Impel Down has served as the World Government's ultimate prison for generations, holding everyone from small-time pirates to world-threatening criminals. Its reputation was built on zero successful escapes - until Shiki the Golden Lion became the first to escape 20 years before the main story by cutting off his own legs. The prison's six levels create a hierarchy of suffering: Level 1 for minor criminals, descending to Level 6 where the world's worst are kept in eternal darkness. A hidden Level 5.5 'Newkama Land' was secretly carved out by prisoners led by Emporio Ivankov. When Luffy learned Ace was being held in Level 6 before execution, he convinced Hancock to smuggle him in. The infiltration turned into chaos as Luffy freed prisoners including Buggy, Mr. 3, Crocodile, Jinbe, and Ivankov. Magellan nearly killed Luffy with poison until Ivankov's hormones saved him. Bon Clay made the ultimate sacrifice, staying behind to open the Gates of Justice. The mass breakout included Level 6 prisoners and culminated in Luffy's crew escaping on a Marine battleship. The event shattered Impel Down's perfect security record and released some of the world's most dangerous criminals.
culture: Impel Down operates on absolute hierarchy and cruelty. Guards are brutal enforcers who show no mercy. The Jailer Beasts (Awakened Zoan users) patrol as mindless monsters. Magellan rules with poison-laced authority, spending most of his time on the toilet due to his fruit's side effects. Vice Warden Hannyabal openly desires to become Chief Warden and incompetently tries to prove himself. The culture is one of despair - prisoners are meant to suffer until death. However, Newkama Land represents rebellion - a hidden paradise where Ivankov's ideology of freedom and transformation thrives. The 'Okama Way' clashes hilariously with the prison's grimness. Guards are deathly afraid of Magellan's poison and Saldeath's Blue Gorillas. Torture is normalized. Escape is considered impossible, making Luffy's invasion and the mass breakout an unprecedented humiliation.
transportation: Accessed via the Gates of Justice, connected to Enies Lobby and Marine Headquarters by special government ships. The main entrance features a massive elevator system descending through all six levels. Prisoners are transported via the Tarai Current system. Internal transportation includes staircases between levels, prisoner transport cages, and hidden passages. The Marine battleship was stolen during the breakout. Newkama Land has secret passages throughout the prison.

cuisine: Magellan's seafood platters (spends hours on toilet after)
Ivankov's Energy Hormones (not food but sustains)
traditions: Magellan's daily diarrhea from poison absorption
quirks: Magellan on toilet 10 hours a day
Buggy pretending to be tough while terrified

notablePeople.name: Magellan
notablePeople.description: The demon-like Chief Warden with the Poison-Poison Fruit. Can produce various types of deadly poison from his body. Nearly invincible inside Impel Down. Spends 10 hours a day on the toilet due to poisoning himself by eating poisoned food. Deeply loyal to justice but devastated when the prison break occurs on his watch.

notablePeople.name: Hannyabal
notablePeople.description: The incompetent but well-meaning Vice Warden who openly admits he wants Magellan's job. Despite his bumbling nature and constant failures, he genuinely cares about preventing evil from escaping. His speech about protecting innocent people from escaped criminals showed his true heroism.

notablePeople.name: Emporio Ivankov
notablePeople.description: The 'Miracle Person' with the Horm-Horm Fruit that can manipulate hormones. A Revolutionary Army commander who was secretly imprisoned in Level 5 but created the hidden Newkama Land. Flamboyant, powerful, and loyal to Dragon (Luffy's father). 'HEEHAW!' is his catchphrase.

notablePeople.name: Bon Clay (Mr. 2)
notablePeople.description: Former Baroque Works agent who became Luffy's dear friend. His Clone-Clone Fruit let him impersonate Magellan to help the escape. Made the ultimate sacrifice by staying behind to open the Gates of Justice, allowing everyone to escape while he was recaptured. A true swan of friendship.

notablePeople.name: Jinbe
notablePeople.description: A whale shark fish-man and former Warlord imprisoned for refusing to fight Whitebeard. Master of Fish-Man Karate. Became Luffy's ally and eventual crew member. Broke out to help save Ace and join the Marineford War.

notablePeople.name: Crocodile
notablePeople.description: The former Warlord defeated by Luffy in Alabasta, imprisoned in Level 6. Freed by Luffy and reluctantly joined the breakout and Marineford battle. His Sand-Sand powers returned when free. Prideful and calculating.

notablePeople.name: Buggy the Clown
notablePeople.description: Luffy's old enemy who accidentally became a hero during the breakout. His Bara-Bara Fruit and dumb luck made prisoners think he was powerful. Freed many Level 2 prisoners who became his followers. Became a Warlord later due to this.

notablePeople.name: Mr. 3 (Galdino)
notablePeople.description: Former Baroque Works agent whose Wax-Wax Fruit became crucial. Created wax armor to protect against Magellan's poison. Became key to escaping Level 4. Reluctant ally.

notablePeople.name: Monkey D. Luffy
notablePeople.description: Infiltrated Impel Down with Hancock's help to rescue Ace. His presence triggered the greatest prison break in history. Nearly died from Magellan's poison but was saved by Ivankov's hormones.

notablePeople.name: Saldeath
notablePeople.description: The devilish-looking chief guard who commands the Blue Gorillas on Level 4. Wields a trident and takes sadistic pleasure in tormenting prisoners.

notablePeople.name: Sadi
notablePeople.description: The sadistic chief jailer who commands the Jailer Beasts (Awakened Zoan guards). Takes pleasure in torturing prisoners with her whip. Attractive but terrifying.

notablePeople.name: Shiryu
notablePeople.description: A swordsman as strong as Magellan, imprisoned for excessive killing of prisoners. Freed during the chaos, he killed many guards and eventually joined Blackbeard's crew. Invisible-Invisible Fruit user later.

notablePeople.name: Domino
notablePeople.description: The cool and composed assistant who helps manage the prison. More competent than Hannyabal but overshadowed during the chaos.

notablePeople.name: Inazuma
notablePeople.description: A Revolutionary Army officer and Ivankov's right hand. The Snip-Snip Fruit lets them turn their hands into giant scissors that can cut anything like paper. Can switch between male and female forms.

notablePeople.name: Daz Bones (Mr. 1)
notablePeople.description: Crocodile's most loyal subordinate with the Dice-Dice Fruit. Freed from Level 4 and followed Crocodile through the breakout and Marineford.

subLocations.name: Level 1 - Crimson Hell
subLocations.description: The first level featuring a forest of blade-like grass and trees that constantly cut prisoners. Relatively mild compared to lower levels. Where Buggy and Mr. 3 were initially held.

subLocations.name: Level 2 - Wild Beast Hell
subLocations.description: Features various deadly beasts including Basilisks, Sphinx, and Manticores. The Jailer Beasts patrol here. Prisoners must survive constant animal attacks.

subLocations.name: Level 3 - Starvation Hell
subLocations.description: An arid desert level where prisoners receive minimal food and water. The heat from Level 4 below makes it unbearably hot. Prisoners slowly die of thirst and hunger.

subLocations.name: Level 4 - Blazing Hell
subLocations.description: A massive pool of boiling blood surrounded by intense flames. The heat is unbearable and prisoners are burned alive. Saldeath and his Blue Gorillas oversee torture here.

subLocations.name: Level 5 - Freezing Hell
subLocations.description: A frozen wasteland where prisoners slowly freeze to death. Filled with wolves. The extreme cold contrasts with Level 4's heat. Many prisoners die before execution.

subLocations.name: Level 5.5 - Newkama Land
subLocations.description: The secret level carved between Levels 5 and 6 by Ivankov and Inazuma. A colorful paradise where escaped prisoners live freely following the Okama Way. Hidden from guards.

subLocations.name: Level 6 - Eternal Hell
subLocations.description: The lowest level where the world's most dangerous criminals are kept. Prisoners here are erased from history. Includes former Warlords, legendary pirates, and world threats. Ace was held here before execution.

subLocations.name: Gates of Justice
subLocations.description: The massive gates controlling access to Impel Down, Marine HQ, and Enies Lobby. Bon Clay sacrificed himself operating the control room to open them for the escapees.

items.name: Poison-Poison Fruit Powers
items.description: Magellan's ability to create various types of deadly poison. His Hydra poison is nearly instantly fatal. Makes him almost unbeatable in close combat.

items.name: Horm-Horm Fruit Powers
items.description: Ivankov's power to manipulate hormones. Can heal, energize, change gender, and create various effects. Used to save Luffy from poison twice.

items.name: Tension Hormones
items.description: Ivankov's hormones that saved Luffy's life by helping him fight off Magellan's poison. Took 10 hours and reduced Luffy's lifespan but saved him.

items.name: Emporio Healing Hormones
items.description: Secondary hormone treatment that gave Luffy energy to fight despite his injuries. Another lifespan reduction but necessary.

items.name: Mr. 3's Wax Armor
items.description: Wax coating that protects against Magellan's poison. Crucial for the escape through Level 4. Can be shaped into armor.

items.name: Seastone Handcuffs
items.description: Special handcuffs used throughout the prison to nullify Devil Fruit powers. Standard equipment for dangerous prisoners.

items.name: Den Den Mushi Network
items.description: The prison's internal communication system. Used to coordinate guards and report emergencies during the breakout chaos.

items.name: Elevator System
items.description: The massive elevator connecting all prison levels. Became a battleground during the escape attempt.

items.name: Stolen Marine Battleship
items.description: The Marine ship stolen by the escapees to flee Impel Down and head to Marineford. Carried Luffy's alliance.

items.name: Ace's Execution Notice
items.description: The notice that brought Luffy to Impel Down in the first place. Announced Ace's public execution at Marineford.

factions.name: Impel Down Staff
factions.description: The World Government employees tasked with containing the world's worst criminals. Overwhelmed during the mass breakout.

factions.name: Revolutionary Army (Newkama Land)
factions.description: Revolutionary commanders secretly operating from Level 5.5, helping Luffy escape and joining his cause.

factions.name: Luffy's Breakout Alliance
factions.description: The chaotic alliance formed during the infiltration, becoming a mass of freed prisoners fighting to escape.

factions.name: Level 6 Breakout Group
factions.description: The world's most dangerous criminals freed by Luffy, forming an uneasy alliance for the escape and Marineford.

factions.name: Blackbeard Pirates (Infiltrators)
factions.description: Blackbeard's crew who infiltrated Level 6 during the chaos to recruit powerful criminals for his crew.

events: Luffy learns Ace will be executed
Hancock smuggles Luffy into Impel Down
Learn Ace already transferred to Marineford
Decision to break out and reach Marineford
Mr. 3's wax armor saves from poison
Ivankov second hormone injection for Luffy
Shiryu released, kills guards, joins Blackbeard
Hannyabal's heroic speech about protecting innocents
Mr. 3 and Luffy's fire combine to break through
Reach Gates of Justice control room
Bon Clay's ultimate sacrifice - stays behind
Opens gates for everyone else to escape
Mass escape on stolen Marine battleship
Head toward Marineford for the war

quickFacts: First successful mass breakout in history
Bon Clay's ultimate sacrifice for friends
Luffy poisoned twice, saved by Ivankov
Buggy accidentally becomes prison break hero
Ace already moved before Luffy arrived

videos.title: Bon Clay's Sacrifice - Opening the Gates
videos.description: The heartbreaking moment where Bon Clay stays behind to open the Gates of Justice, sacrificing his freedom for his friends.

videos.title: Luffy vs Magellan - Poison Battle
videos.description: Luffy's desperate fight against Magellan's overwhelming poison powers, ending in near-death.

videos.title: Luffy and Allies vs Blackbeard Pirates
videos.description: The chaotic battle as Luffy's breakout alliance clashes with Blackbeard's infiltrators in Level 6.
